section.name: Introduction
section.p: This document describes the process of creating and maintaining translated texts for BreakTest in languages other than English. BreakTest inherits many resource keys from Apache JMeter for compatibility, so some keys and package paths still contain jmeter.
Translation changes are contributed through GitHub pull requests. Open an issue first if you plan a large translation update or a new language, so maintainers can help avoid duplicate work.

section.name: Obtaining the current texts
section.p: Fork and clone the BreakTest repository:
The translatable text files are stored as Java properties files in the source tree. Use git grep or your IDE to find the locale you want to update.

section.name: Editing translations
section.p: You can edit translations with your preferred editor, IDE, or a properties-file translation tool such as i18nEdit. Keep files encoded and escaped consistently with the existing file you are editing.

When updating an existing locale, preserve keys that are still used by BreakTest. When a label intentionally remains a compatibility term, such as JMeterThread or jmeter.properties, do not translate it as product branding.

section.name: Testing translations
section.p: Run the relevant checks before opening a pull request:
For GUI text changes, also start BreakTest locally and inspect the affected dialogs or components.

section.name: Submitting your translations
section.p: Create a topic branch, commit your changes, push to your fork, and open a pull request against BreakTest. Include the language, the area you changed, and any screenshots that make review easier.
